titlestmt.titleproper: Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection8107
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-11-13 18:25:25 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection
did.abstract: Flyers, posters, magazines, files, and zines relating to American punk and alternative culture collected or created by Aaron Cometbus, including his own work as a writer and musician.
did.physdesc: Flyers, printed material, posters, photographs, correspondence, and ephemera.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Aaron Elliott, better known as Aaron Cometbus, has published Cometbus magazine since 1981. He is also an accomplished novelist, lyricist, and drummer.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Flyers, posters, magazines, files, and zines relating to American punk and alternative culture collected or created by Aaron Cometbus, including his own work as a writer and musician. The archive contains hundreds of fanzines and small magazines, plus original manuscripts and paste-ups for Cometbus and related publishing and distribution records. Board games, business cards, set lists, and venue histories provide a personal, behind-the-scenes view of punk both as music and as a movement, from 1980 to the present.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Introduction to the collection written by Aaron Elliot can be found in Box 1, Folder A.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection, #8107. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

emph.ref: Series I. Punk and Underground Press Materials
Series II. Event Flyers and Posters
Series V. Band Files and Related Materials
Series VIII. Letters to Cometbus Magazine

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Audio visual media that has been digitized: TR-15991.

did.unittitle: "Anarchy in the Archives" edition of Cometbus
Series I. Punk and Underground Press Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes fanzines, magazines, newsletters, comics, and newspapers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The first two subseries are arranged geographically starting with locations in the Bay Area and then alphabetically by title within each location.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: "Creator" is used throughout Series I to denote the author, editor, or publisher of a work.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Folders previously numbered 4-100 and 4-101 have been reprocessed and are now numbered 17-9 and 17-10.

did.unittitle: Angels We Have Heard While High

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1 No. 1 (2 copies), 4 (2 copies), 5, 7, 11, 14-15, 18 (3 copies), 19, 20 (2 copies), 21, 22 (2 copies), 25, 27 (2 copies), 29, 33-35; Vol. 2 No. 23; Vol. 3 No. 1, 3 (2 copies), 4, 5 (2 copies), 6, 8

did.unittitle: Boy, Does High School Ever Suck
Epi-Logue - The Zine of Epicenter Zone's Demise

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two issues. Donor note: This fanzine shouldn't be taken as a representative example of the form, the scene, or the time. "Punkette" was an alien phrase then as now, and what made this mag interesting was how totally off the editor seemed, perhaps a 45-year old shut-in reporting on a youth culture that excited him but which he knew little about. Sort of a precursor to the internet!

did.unittitle: I Gotta Get This Off My Chest
Monthly Independent Tribune Times Journal Post Gazette News Chronicle Bulletin, The
Murder Can Be Fun Presents: Obscure Crime Books
Notes from the Dead Letter Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 1 and Treason/Out Magazine Sunday Supplement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donor note: the 1st Berkeley punk zine

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Performing Arts Social Society (San Francisco, Calif.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donor note: A S.F. free-love-and-computers cult rebranded their journal as an alt. rock mag briefly, before their break up. The result is strange indeed: mostly a failed recruiting tool, but with a disarming Joey Ramone interview in this issue.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Untitled zine; title devised by processing staff.

Spitboy Rule: Tales of a Female Punk Band, The

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donor note: Early zine version of the later book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donor note: Before the record label, when S.P. was still a fanzine from Olympia.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Issues were bound with rubber bands. All rubber bands removed by processing staff.

This is Not a Travel Journal

scopecontent.p: Vol. 2 No. 3 (No. 7), Vol. 2 No. 4 (No. 8), Vol. 2 No. 5 (No. 8)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donor note: These are originals for an East Bay comics anthology, edited by Chris Applegren, which was never released, circa 1992.

scopecontent.p: Includes notes about article on punk ephemera for Nuts zine, photocopies of business cards, Nuts zine

did.unittitle: Series II. Event Flyers and Posters

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Nearly every flyer in Boxes 9 and 10 have been scanned and are available through the Cornell Library Digital Collections portal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder labeled as "Not Punk but Kinda Cool/Odd"

did.unittitle: This is you and it doesn't matter flyer

Venues Klu-Nov (excluding Mabuhay Gardens and New Method)
Venues Or-Stu (excluding Rock on Broadway, Ruthie's Inn, Sound of Music and The Stone)
Venues Tam-You (excluding Tool and Die, Twilight Zone, and the V.I.S. Club)
Multiple Venue Locations (on the same flyer)
Cleveland Bound Death Sentence and Crimpshrine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains double-sided "Rock Against Reagan 1983" and "World Cannabis March on the U.N." poster.

did.unittitle: The flyers in their natural habitat: My room

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of Aaron in his bedroom with flyer-covered walls.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: U.K. Subs, Anti-Nowhere League. Note from donor: This caused a huge laugh. Of course, hardcore was particularly and uniquely American at that point, and certainly wasn't represented by two geezer/biker UK bands, even if the two bands happened to be pretty good.

did.unittitle: Blackout Books - bookstore and info shop
Crowd surfing and moshing is not advised

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handmade poster from a Green Day concert

Das Kriminal, War Torn Babies, Astrid Oto, Crap Factory

scopecontent.p: Poster for a show at The HQ

scopecontent.p: Includes flyers, calendars, and fifth anniversary zine.

scopecontent.p: Independent, fan-made calendars of upcoming shows and cultural events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original flyers, posters, handwritten instructions for show and venue positions; donor note: "the club we ran after the demise of the Headquarters"

did.unittitle: Series V. Band Files and Related Materials

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Many of the event flyers in this series have been scanned and are available through the Cornell Library Digital Collections portal.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.processinfo: Folders previously numbered 5-54, 5-55, 5-56, 5-57, and 5-59 have been reprocessed and are now numbered 17-22, 17-23, 17-24, 17-25, and 17-26.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Folders previously numbered 5-54, 5-55, 5-56, 5-57, and 5-59 have been reprocessed and are now numbered 17-22, 17-23, 17-24, 17-25, and 17-26.

scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten lyrics, posters, flyers, photographs, patches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes promotional photos, posters, correspondence and Aaron Cometbus note about Spinal Tap promotions

scopecontent.p: Includes flyers, posters, photographs, patches, Aaron Cometbus notes, and lyric /info booklet for the band A State of Mind

scopecontent.p: Includes Aaron Cometbus notes, tape cassette inserts

scopecontent.p: Includes drafts and notes for compilation tape liner notes about the Bay Area's punk music scene

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains flyers and an advertisement for the "Live '84" cassette.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an introduction by Aaron Cometbus, photos, letters, drawings.

scopecontent.p: Includes flyers, photographs, Lookout Records contract

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original record lyric insert artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a sketch of the origin and meaning of the original Crass logo made by Dave King, a photocopy of a letter by Joy de Vivre, and a stencilled Crass logo made by Aaron Cometbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letter from Joy De Vivre and Crass leaflets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters, flyers, merchandise signs, stickers, correspondence, newspaper clippings, Lookout Records contract, photos, tour booklet

scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings, photos, lyric booklets, paste-ups for lyric sheets and tape inserts.

scopecontent.p: Includes demo tape cover and lyric sheet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Aaron Cometbus note and advertisement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains news clippings, postcards, and photographs.

scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of newspaper clippings, photograph, dolphin image, poster, tour flyer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes stickers, photographs, flyers, tour schedule

did.unittitle: Two handmade posters from Green Day concert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Selling L.P.s, T-shirts, socks, and Cometbus fanzines. Posters written on the back of a flyer for Monsula and Sawhorse Summer Tour 1990.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes interview and press packet from first national tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original handwritten lettering for LP.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sheets and booklets from Bikini Kill, Operation Ivy, Crucifix, Atrocity, Trial, Econochrist, Filth, Treason, The Gr'ups, The Quails, and Ten Tall Men, No. 47

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donor note: These aren't record inserts, they are from a time when most bands handed out lyric sheets at shows.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newsletters, posters, flyers, photographs, clippings and a copy of the MTX fan club zine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photocopied cassette insert and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a donor note by Aaron Cometbus and photocopies of a check written out to him from the band.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers, newsletters, and original artwork by Jesse Michaels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten lyrics, reviews, individual disbursement reports, posters, flyers, clippings, correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Peace Energy Action Cooperation Evolution (PEACE)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers, photos, letters, and artwork paste-ups.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains introduction to the file by Aaron Cometbus and a handwritten note by Rick Rubin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains original artwork and lyric sheet paste-ups.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containers flyers and a Dillinger Four set list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1988 issue of Empire Monthly, newsletters, posters, a letter from Ben Weasel, and an introduction to the file by Aaron Cometbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poster sized set list from a show at Club Foot in 1984

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains flyers and a lyric sheet paste-up.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, flyers, set lists, news clippings, and posters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains contacts from several different band tours, including Crimpshrine, Sweet Baby Jesus, and Green Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes zines, programs, record catalogs, pamphlets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings, stickers, photographs, flyers, Aaron Cometbus notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes cards from Aaron Cometbus to others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reproductions and original illustrations by Ithaca-born artist Bobby Madness, as well as an introduction by Aaron Cometbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes business cards, Nuts zine, and note from Aaron Cometbus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ticket stubs, clippings, flyers, stickers, photographs and an invitation to Jello Biafra and Therese Soder's wedding. Also includes a Public Humiliation lighter (MU-1880), Isocracy matchbook (MU-1881), and Captain Crunch guitar pick (MU-1882).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles, correspondence, inspection report, notice of nuisance, notice of complaint notice of hearing, matchbooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter describing the mid-1980's Berkeley practice of hedgediving

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains flyers, photographs, and news clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes buttons from Dead Kennedys, White Flag, Psychedelic Furs, Trial, Vicious Circle, KALX, Flipside Video, The Witnesses, The Contractions, and Aaardvark's Odd Ark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes KSAN 95FM poster and Free Radio Ashville sticker and flyer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes KSAN 95FM poster and Free Radio Asheville flyer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Community calendar of upcoming cultural events and historical dates, started by Aaron Cometbus and edited by a revolving cast, named after community hub, Subterranean Books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains cards from Wisconsin(1995) and the Superchunk (Chunk)/Seaweed/Geek tour(1990)

did.unittitle: Exhibit of artwork by Joanne Segal Brandford, Diana de Filippi, Lillian Elliott, Pat Hickman, Martie Holmer, Sarah Redfield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lillian Elliot is Aaron Elliot's mother.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.processinfo: Folder previously numbered 5-49 has been reprocessed and is now numbered 17-21.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Folder previously numbered 5-49 has been reprocessed and is now numbered 17-21.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes board game and related materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes board game materials and Aaron Cometbus note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains paste-ups for the game and issue of Apology containing essay about the game by Aaron Cometbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "Songs of Wrath and Optimism" by Astrid Oto, "Untitled" by Astrid Oto, "House Band Feud" by The Blank Fight, "Untitled" by Cleveland Bound Death Sentence, "Sleep, What's That? EP" by Crimpshrine, "On the Waterfront EP" by Crybaby MacArthur, "Untitled" by Pinhead Gunpowder, and "Redmond Shooting Stars" by Redmond Shooting Stars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an introduction by Aaron Cometbus and the cassette tapes: "Banned in R.C." by Blatz, "Rokken' Like Dokken'" by The Blottos, "Live at the Metropolis" by Brap Smears, "Brent's T.V." by Brent's T.V., "Through Our Eyes" by Complete Disorder, "Untitled" by Crimpshrine, "Driller Killers" by Driller Killers, "Arbgabdo Baby!" by Fleshies, "Eartraining for Corporates" by Harbinger, "Jüke @ Gilman" by Jüke, "Untitled" by Maniax, "Bloody Marys…" by Premarital Sex, "It's Not Just a Band…It's a Lifestyle" by Soup, and "Pug's Not Dead" by Zombie Dogs. Also contains the compilation tapes "Gimme Cooties," "Absolutely Zippo Presents...'Time Capsule'," "Every Dog Has its Blues: The Bands of Lucky Dog," and "We Got Glue."

did.unittitle: Series VIII. Letters to Cometbus Magazine

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by letter date or post-marked envelope date where available.

did.unittitle: Introduction to the Letters to Cometbus Magazine pt. 1, 1981-1989

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early letters stapled together with corresponding envelopes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes introduction to the file by Aaron Cometbus.

did.unittitle: Introduction to Letters to Cometbus Magazine, pt. 2, 1990-99

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donor note: This letter serves as a funny introduction to the fanzine section, and even the archive as a whole.
